inputs.entityStatus: Wyoming DAO LLC, legally operating, EIN issued, registered agent active
inputs.currentFinancialInfrastructure: None — zero bank account, credit profile, D-U-N-S, vendor accounts
inputs.annualBaselineOperatingCosts: $400 (registered agent, mail forwarding, annual report, filing fees, API/gas)
inputs.taxStructure: Single-member disregarded entity, Schedule C, Wyoming (no state income tax)
inputs.humanExecutorPriorityOrder: Bank → D-U-N-S → tradelines → store credit → business credit cards → ancillary

corpusSources.relevance: Capital allocation philosophy: deploy resources where they compound most effectively; avoid permanent loss of capital; 'mistakes fade away, winners can forever blossom.' Applicable to sequencing financial infrastructure investments.
corpusSources.citedPassage: 'Never risk permanent loss of capital.' And: 'a single winning decision can make a breathtaking difference over time.'

corpusSources.relevance: Long-term compounding orientation; importance of building durable financial infrastructure before seeking returns; conservative balance sheet management enables optionality.
corpusSources.citedPassage: 'Berkshire benefits from an unusual constancy and clarity of purpose... our allegiance will always be to our country and our shareholders.'

corpusSources.relevance: Capital allocation as primary management responsibility; float as structural advantage; cost of capital matters. Banking and credit infrastructure function analogously to float — free or low-cost capital access enables compounding.
corpusSources.citedPassage: 'An insurance business has value if its cost of float over time is less than the cost the company would otherwise incur to obtain funds.' Applies by analogy: business credit at near-zero cost (net-30 tradelines paid on time) creates optionality without capital drain.

corpusSources.relevance: Principle 1 (Clearly Defined Boundaries) directly applies: financial infrastructure defines the DAO's operational boundary. Without a bank account, the entity cannot transact. Principle 4 (Monitoring): API-accessible banking enables programmatic governance monitoring of financial flows.
corpusSources.citedPassage: 'The boundaries of the resource system and the individuals or households with rights to use the resource must be clearly defined... Without clear boundaries, free-riders cannot be excluded, and contributors have no assurance that they will capture the benefits of their efforts.'

corpusSources.relevance: DAO governance requires operational infrastructure. The paper's discussion of participation constraints and incentive compatibility applies: governance systems require solvent operational infrastructure or participation collapses. Financial infrastructure is a precondition for incentive-compatible DAO governance.
corpusSources.citedPassage: 'Is equilibrium participation in the stablecoin sustainable? This requires that incentives among all participants... lead to a mutually profitable equilibrium of participating.' Operational solvency (bank account, credit) is a precondition for any participation equilibrium.

analysis.reasoning: RECOMMENDATION: Mercury (Option A). RATIONALE: The Root Thesis Maxim requires building 'sovereign, self-sustaining systems that compound human agency over generational timescales.' A bank account is the single most foundational infrastructure element — nothing else in this proposal is achievable without it. Mercury scores highest on all evaluation criteria: (1) $0/month cost (mandatory), (2) API access for programmatic transaction queries directly enables future governance-engine integration — this is uniquely aligned with the Maxim's emphasis on algorithmic sovereignty, (3) DAO/crypto-adjacent customer base means lower account closure risk than traditional banks, (4) Treasury product for idle cash provides future compounding optionality, (5) FDIC insured up to $5M via sweep network exceeds the entity's near-term balance needs. OPTION SCORES: Mercury 0.92 — highest alignment, API enables governance transparency thesis, crypto-friendly reduces existential account-closure risk. Relay 0.78 — solid for budgeting discipline via Profit First, but no API access and fewer integrations limits future governance-engine integration. Bluevine 0.72 — interest-earning deposits are attractive but less relevant at current balance levels; less DAO-friendly. Traditional bank 0.45 — monthly fees, minimum balances, documentation overhead, and crypto-hostile posture create significant alignment risks. RISKS OF MERCURY: Mercury is a fintech-model bank (partner bank FDIC insurance rather than direct charter). While FDIC protection is equivalent, Mercury has had past scrutiny from regulators. The DAO should monitor Mercury's regulatory status. CONTINGENCY: If Mercury declines the account due to DAO structure, Relay is the backup — its multiple checking accounts enable future budget segregation consistent with Ostrom Principle 1 (defined boundaries for resource allocation). MAXIM ALIGNMENT: Mercury's API access enables the transparency thesis — every transaction becomes programmatically auditable, directly advancing the governance intelligence layer's operational mandate. Score: 0.92.

RECOMMENDATION: Expedited registration ($50, ~5-7 business days, Option A). RATIONALE: The $50 cost must be evaluated against the compounding value of 3 additional weeks of credit-building timeline. Buffett's letters emphasize that capital allocation errors compound in both directions — delaying a foundational infrastructure step for $50 when the entire credit-building program's value is measured in thousands of dollars of future access is penny-wise and pound-foolish. QUANTITATIVE ANALYSIS: The PAYDEX score requires 3 paid net-30 tradelines. Standard registration delays the entire sequence by ~3 weeks. On a 12-month horizon, this means: (a) tradeline applications begin ~3 weeks later, (b) first PAYDEX score posts ~3 weeks later, (c) store credit eligibility (month 3-4 target) slips to month 4-5, (d) business credit card eligibility (month 5-8) slips to month 6-9. The compounding effect of a 3-week delay early in the sequence propagates to each subsequent stage. Against a $50 cost, this is a clear positive expected value trade. OPTION SCORES: Expedited 0.85, Standard 0.68. RISK ASSESSMENT: Minimal. D&B registration is a standard business process. The expedited fee is non-refundable if the process encounters issues, but D&B is a well-established credit bureau and registration failures are extremely rare. The primary risk is D&B requesting additional documentation for a DAO LLC structure — prepare EIN confirmation, Articles of Organization, and registered agent verification in advance. MAXIM ALIGNMENT: Credit infrastructure compounds over time. Accelerating the start date by 3 weeks has a disproportionate effect on the 12-month horizon. Score: 0.85.

analysis.description: Decision Domain 3 — Net-30 Tradeline Strategy: Recommendation
analysis.reasoning: RECOMMENDATION: Open 3 initial tradelines in sequenced order: (1) Uline, (2) Quill, (3) Crown Office Supplies. Add Strategic Network Solutions and Shirtsy/4Over in months 2-3. RATIONALE: D&B's PAYDEX score requires 3+ reporting tradelines. The goal is to reach a PAYDEX of 80+ (pays on time) as quickly as possible. Strategy requires: (a) opening accounts in sequence to stagger first invoice payments for maximum early PAYDEX impact, (b) purchasing items with genuine business utility to maintain clean deduction posture, (c) staying within the $200/month discretionary budget. VENDOR ANALYSIS: Uline (Score 0.88) — reliable D&B reporter, wide product range (shipping supplies directly relevant to any physical business operations, labels, storage for equipment/documents). Order minimum ~$25-50 achievable. Purchase recommendation: shipping labels, document storage boxes, cable organizers for equipment. Quill (Score 0.87) — reports to D&B, extremely broad product range of legitimate technology company deductions (USB drives, cables, surge protectors, printer supplies, desk accessories). Low minimums. Shirtsy/4Over (Score 0.82) — branded collateral (business cards, stickers) creates legitimate marketing expense and physical collateral for the publication/launch strategy referenced in Human Executor notes. Crown Office Supplies (Score 0.80) — lower minimums, focused product range, reliable reporter. Strategic Network Solutions (Score 0.78) — tech accessories directly relevant to technology company operations. BUDGET CEILING RECOMMENDATION: $150/month maximum across all tradeline accounts (leaving $50/month buffer within the $200 discretionary budget). Target $30-50 per vendor per cycle. Every purchase must be: (a) functionally usable for DAO operations, (b) a legitimate Schedule C deduction, (c) paid on the net-30 terms (not early, not late — PAYDEX rewards on-time payment). CRITICAL COMPLIANCE NOTE: Do not pay tradelines early. Early payment does not improve PAYDEX and reduces the 'reporting period' signal. Pay on day 28-30 of the net-30 window consistently. MAXIM ALIGNMENT: Building a PAYDEX score is foundational to future credit access, which enables the DAO to engage contractors, purchase equipment, and fund operations without personal account exposure — directly advancing entity sovereignty. Score: 0.83.

analysis.description: Decision Domain 4 — Store Credit Accounts (Month 3-4): Recommendation
analysis.reasoning: RECOMMENDATION: Priority order: (1) Dell Business Credit, (2) Amazon Business Line of Credit, (3) Staples Business Advantage. DEFER Best Buy Business unless equipment need materializes. RATIONALE: Store credit accounts at month 3-4 represent the first credit products requiring a PAYDEX score for approval. The priority ordering is driven by: (a) reporting breadth (multi-bureau reporters provide more credit profile diversification), (b) relevance to actual DAO operations, (c) credit line potential. Dell Business Credit (Score 0.88) — reports to both D&B and Experian Business, significantly strengthening the credit profile. Technology purchases (laptop, monitor, peripherals) are the most defensible large capital expenditure for a technology DAO. Section 179 expensing applies. A Dell account at month 3-4 positions for future equipment acquisition without personal credit exposure. Amazon Business Line of Credit (Score 0.85) — reports to D&B, extremely broad utility for business purchases, convenience factor enables just-in-time procurement without float exposure. Staples Business Advantage (Score 0.78) — office supply continuity, modest credit line, but provides bureau diversity. Best Buy Business (Deferred, Score 0.65 at this stage) — overlap with Dell for electronics purchases; defer until a specific equipment acquisition need materializes. APPLICATION TIMING: Apply for Dell at week 10-12 (after 3 paid tradelines have reported). Apply for Amazon at week 12-14. Apply for Staples at week 14-16. Do not apply for multiple store accounts on the same day — space applications 2 weeks apart to minimize inquiry clustering. RISK: At $0 revenue, some store credit issuers may request personal guarantee or decline without revenue history. Dell Business Credit is known to approve on PAYDEX alone with sufficient tradeline history. Have a fallback plan: if Dell declines, pivot to Office Depot Business or Newegg Business Credit. MAXIM ALIGNMENT: Dell Business Credit in particular advances the Maxim by enabling capital equipment acquisition (computer, monitor, peripherals) on business credit rather than personal funds — a direct sovereignty-building mechanism. Score: 0.83.

analysis.description: Decision Domain 5 — Business Credit Card Strategy (Month 5-8): Recommendation
analysis.reasoning: RECOMMENDATION: Sequenced approach — (1) American Express Blue Business Cash first (month 5-6), (2) Capital One Spark Cash second (month 6-7), defer Chase cards until month 8+ or until revenue materializes. RATIONALE: The key decision variable here is personal credit impact vs. business-only reporting, and the optimal sequencing depends on PAYDEX score strength at application time. American Express Blue Business Cash (Score 0.88) — reports to business bureaus only after approval (does not appear on personal credit report post-approval, though a hard pull may occur during application). 2% cash back on first $50K is directly valuable for DAO operational purchases. No annual fee. The Amex business relationship is a long-term strategic asset — Amex's lending history, charge-off tolerance, and product suite (future Plum Card, Business Platinum) justify establishing this relationship early. Capital One Spark Cash (Score 0.82) — reports to business bureaus, 2% unlimited cash back, lower approval threshold than Chase/Amex at this stage without revenue, good starter card. Chase Ink Business Cash (Score 0.75, DEFERRED) — 5% categories are attractive, but Chase's '5/24 rule' and revenue scrutiny make this more appropriate at month 8+ when credit profile is more developed. More importantly, Chase Ink reports to personal credit bureaus — at $0 revenue with hobby loss risk (see Domain 7), minimizing personal credit profile growth until the entity has demonstrable profit intent is prudent. Chase Ink Business Preferred (Score 0.65, DEFERRED) — $95 annual fee requires demonstrated spend velocity to justify. CRITICAL NOTE ON PERSONAL CREDIT IMPACT: At zero revenue, Chase products that report to personal credit may raise underwriting flags during future mortgage or personal credit applications. The Maxim emphasizes sovereign systems — building business credit that does not burden the member's personal financial profile is aligned with this principle. MAXIM ALIGNMENT: Business credit cards that report to business bureaus only advance entity sovereignty by establishing credit capacity independent of the member's personal profile. Score: 0.80.

analysis.description: Decision Domain 6 — Sixt Business Account & Vehicle Strategy: Recommendation
analysis.reasoning: RECOMMENDATION: Register business account now (Option A: $0 cost), subscribe only when/if operational need materializes. Explicit trigger conditions required before subscription. RATIONALE: The $0 cost to register a Sixt Business account is a pure option — it creates future value without current cost. Buffett's letters consistently identify low-cost optionality as intrinsically valuable. The Sixt Business account registration is essentially a free call option on corporate vehicle pricing. REGISTRATION: Approve immediately. Cost: $0. Time: ~10 minutes. Outcome: locks in corporate pricing tier. SUBSCRIPTION ANALYSIS (for future reference): The Sixt+ subscription at ~$552-611/month (with corporate discount) is financially justified ONLY when: (a) the member requires monthly vehicle access for documented business purposes (client meetings, site visits, equipment transport), AND (b) total monthly cost is less than alternative vehicle access methods (car ownership + insurance, standard rental, rideshare), AND (c) business-use mileage can be documented at >50% for Schedule C deduction. At $0 revenue and no current operational need for regular vehicle access, subscribing now would represent a $552-611/month burn rate against a $400/year baseline operating cost — an increase of approximately 1,600% with no business justification. This clearly fails the Buffett principle of 'never risk permanent loss of capital' on non-mission-critical expenditures. DEFER CONDITIONS: Do not subscribe until (a) first token revenue is recognized, (b) business-use driving exceeds 400 miles/month, or (c) a specific operational contract requiring regular travel is executed. MAXIM ALIGNMENT: Pure optionality at $0 cost scores maximally. Registering without subscribing is optimal. Score: 0.90 for register-only; subscription evaluation deferred pending trigger conditions.

analysis.description: Decision Domain 7 — Tax Optimization & Deduction Strategy: Recommendation
analysis.reasoning: RECOMMENDATION: Conservative deduction posture in 2026, prioritize establishing profit intent before year 3 (2028). DETAILED DEDUCTION ANALYSIS BY CATEGORY: DEFENSIBLE DEDUCTIONS (pursue aggressively): (1) Registered Agent Fee ($125/year) — 100% deductible, directly required by Wyoming statute. (2) Mail Forwarding ($240/year) — 100% deductible as business address service. (3) Annual Report/Filing Fees ($100/year) — 100% deductible state compliance costs. (4) API Costs/Gas Fees — 100% deductible as direct technology operating costs of the DAO's smart contract infrastructure. (5) D-U-N-S Registration ($50 expedited) — 100% deductible as startup/organizational cost. (6) Net-30 Tradeline Purchases (within $150/month ceiling) — deductible to extent items have business utility (shipping supplies, tech accessories, office supplies, branded materials). Maintain purchase logs documenting business purpose for each item. (7) Business Banking Fees (Mercury: $0) — deductible if any incurred. (8) Home Office (conditional) — the proposal notes 'shared workspace constraints.' If the member can document exclusive, regular use of a defined workspace area for DAO business, the simplified method ($5/sq ft up to 300 sq ft = $1,500 max/year) may be available. Do not claim without documented exclusive-use test compliance. HIGH RISK — AVOID OR DOCUMENT CAREFULLY: (1) Vehicle/transportation deductions — only deductible for documented business trips with mileage logs. Without revenue-generating activities requiring travel, these are difficult to defend. (2) Meals — 50% deductible but require documentation of business purpose and attendees. Avoid without strong documentation. (3) Entertainment — substantially eliminated post-2018 TCJA. Avoid entirely. HOBBY LOSS RISK (Section 183) — CRITICAL: The IRS presumes hobby if the activity does not show profit in 3 of 5 consecutive tax years. With $0 revenue in 2026, the clock starts now. RECOMMENDED STRATEGY: (a) Establish a formal revenue timeline — the governance system should produce a Proposal #004 or #005 targeting first token revenue generation no later than Q2 2027. (b) Document every business activity: governance decisions, development work, publication strategy, contractor engagement. The depth of governance documentation (this very proposal system) is strong evidence of profit intent. (c) Maintain a separate, dedicated business checking account (Mercury) from day one — commingling personal and business funds is the single most damaging audit factor. (d) Consider filing a formal business plan as a governance document — the on-chain record of business planning activity constitutes evidence of profit intent. (e) Target first revenue by December 31, 2027 (year 2) to establish profit in at least 1 of the first 2 years, creating momentum toward the 3-of-5 safe harbor. 2026 PROJECTED DEDUCTIONS (conservative estimate): Registered Agent $125 + Mail Forwarding $240 + Annual Report $100 + API/Gas Fees $200 + D-U-N-S $50 + Net-30 Purchases (legitimate portion) $900 + Miscellaneous Business Expenses $200 = approximately $1,815. At a combined federal effective rate of ~22% (single, standard deduction territory), this produces ~$399 in tax savings — modest but establishing the deduction posture. MAXIM ALIGNMENT: Conservative, defensible deduction strategy preserves the DAO's legal standing and audit posture over generational timescales. Aggressive deduction strategies that trigger Section 183 classification would retroactively disallow all deductions and potentially expose the member to penalties — a catastrophic outcome for a bootstrapped entity. Score: 0.85 for conservative posture.

WEEK 1 (Days 1-7): PRIORITY — Banking and D-U-N-S. ACTION 1: Open Mercury business checking account. Required documents: EIN confirmation letter, Articles of Organization, operating agreement, member's government ID, business address (registered agent address acceptable). Expected completion: 1-3 business days. ACTION 2: Register for D-U-N-S number (expedited, $50). Submit business name, address, EIN, business description ('Wyoming DAO LLC providing blockchain-based governance infrastructure and digital asset management services'). ACTION 3: Register Sixt Business Account ($0). Required: business name, EIN, address. ACTION 4: Establish Mercury account as the sole business financial account. Do not use personal accounts for any DAO expenses going forward. WEEK 2 (Days 8-14): Tradeline Account Opening. ACTION 5: Apply for Uline net-30 account. ACTION 6: Apply for Quill (Staples subsidiary) net-30 account. ACTION 7: Apply for Crown Office Supplies net-30 account. Note: Some vendors may require D-U-N-S confirmation before approving. If D-U-N-S not yet confirmed, prioritize Quill and Crown (lower barriers) and follow with Uline upon D-U-N-S confirmation. WEEK 3 (Days 15-21): First Tradeline Purchases. ACTION 8: Place first order with each approved tradeline vendor — $30-50 per vendor, business-utility items only. Document business purpose for each purchase. Total spend: ~$90-150. ACTION 9: Record all purchases in a business expense log. WEEK 4-8 (Days 22-56): Tradeline Seasoning Period. ACTION 10: Pay all tradeline invoices on day 28-30 of net-30 window (not early). ACTION 11: Apply for Strategic Network Solutions net-30 (week 4). ACTION 12: Apply for Shirtsy/4Over print account (week 5) — order branded business cards and stickers for OpenInnovate DAO. ACTION 13: Place second round of orders with original three vendors (week 6). ACTION 14: Monitor D-U-N-S profile for PAYDEX score generation (typically appears after first reported payment). WEEK 9-12 (Days 57-84): Store Credit Applications. ACTION 15: At week 10 (after 3+ tradelines have reported to D-U-N-S): Apply for Dell Business Credit. ACTION 16: At week 12: Apply for Amazon Business Line of Credit. ACTION 17: At week 14: Apply for Staples Business Advantage Credit. MONTHS 5-8 (Days 85-240): Business Credit Card Applications. ACTION 18: At month 5 (PAYDEX 70+): Apply for American Express Blue Business Cash. ACTION 19: At month 6: Apply for Capital One Spark Cash. ACTION 20: Defer Chase products until month 8 minimum, or until first revenue recognized. ONGOING: Monthly governance documentation of all financial activities for audit posture.

BASELINE OPERATING COSTS (pre-existing): Registered Agent $125 + Mail Forwarding $240 + Annual Report $100 + API Costs/Gas $200 = $665/year. NEW INFRASTRUCTURE COSTS: D-U-N-S Expedited $50 (one-time). Net-30 Tradeline Purchases: $150/month × 12 months = $1,800 (items retained as business assets or consumed in operations). Business Banking: $0 (Mercury). Sixt Registration: $0. Store Credit Accounts: Credit lines, not cash outflow (paid monthly from Mercury account — net spend within tradeline budget). Business Credit Cards: Rewards cards, net cash back positive at 2% on qualifying spend. TOTAL 12-MONTH CASH OUTFLOW ABOVE BASELINE: D-U-N-S $50 + Tradeline Purchases $1,800 = $1,850 incremental. TOTAL 12-MONTH ALL-IN COST: $665 (baseline) + $1,850 (new infrastructure) = $2,515. This is well within the $200/month discretionary budget ($2,400/year) plus minor overage covered by baseline operating budget. NET AFTER CREDIT CARD CASH BACK (months 5-12): Assuming $150/month on 2% cash back cards = $3/month = $24 cash back. Net incremental cost net of cash back: $1,826. BUDGET VERDICT: The 12-month financial infrastructure program is fully fundable within stated constraints.

alternativesConsidered.alternative: Delay all financial infrastructure until first revenue
alternativesConsidered.reason_rejected: Creates circular dependency — without bank account and credit profile, the DAO cannot engage contractors, purchase services, or establish vendor relationships needed to generate revenue. Buffett's letters consistently emphasize that foundational infrastructure investment must precede revenue generation, not follow it. The cost of delay (compounding credit timeline) exceeds the cost of action ($1,850 incremental over 12 months). Maxim Alignment: 0.35.

alternativesConsidered.alternative: Traditional bank (Chase Business, US Bank) instead of Mercury
alternativesConsidered.reason_rejected: Traditional banks have documented history of closing DAO LLC accounts without warning due to crypto-adjacent classification. The existential risk of account closure mid-operation — losing all financial infrastructure in a single event — represents a catastrophic failure mode incompatible with the Maxim's emphasis on sovereign, self-sustaining systems. Monthly fees and minimum balance requirements create ongoing drag. API access absence eliminates governance-engine integration potential. Maxim Alignment: 0.45.

alternativesConsidered.alternative: Aggressive deduction strategy including home office, vehicle, and all purchases
alternativesConsidered.reason_rejected: At $0 revenue with shared workspace constraints and no documented business-use mileage, aggressive deductions create Section 183 hobby loss risk that could retroactively disallow ALL deductions across multiple years. The audit exposure and reputational cost to the legal entity far exceeds the marginal tax savings. The Maxim requires generational stability — a tax dispute in year 2 could terminate the entity. Maxim Alignment: 0.30.

alternativesConsidered.alternative: Subscribe to Sixt+ immediately for business vehicle access
alternativesConsidered.reason_rejected: At $0 revenue, $552-611/month subscription represents 1,600% increase over baseline operating cost with no documented business purpose. Pure cash burn without compounding return. Violates Buffett's principle of never deploying capital without expected return exceeding cost. Maxim Alignment: 0.15.

alternativesConsidered.alternative: Apply for Chase Ink Business Cash before Amex in credit card sequence
alternativesConsidered.reason_rejected: Chase reports to personal credit bureau, and Chase's underwriting at $0 revenue is more stringent. Applying too early risks denial and hard inquiry without approval — a negative expected value trade. Additionally, Chase's 5/24 rule means timing matters for long-term relationship value. Deferring to month 8 optimizes both approval odds and personal credit profile management. Maxim Alignment: 0.62 for early Chase vs. 0.80 for Amex-first sequencing.

alternativesConsidered.alternative: Open all five tradeline vendors simultaneously in week 1
alternativesConsidered.reason_rejected: Multiple simultaneous applications create a D&B inquiry pattern that may signal financial distress rather than systematic credit building. Staggered applications over 4-6 weeks appear more organic, maximize the independent PAYDEX reporting events, and allow the DAO to calibrate purchase volumes before committing to all five vendors. Sequenced approach is superior. Maxim Alignment: 0.72 for simultaneous vs. 0.83 for sequenced.

reasoningTree.traceabilityChain: RECOMMENDATION → ACTION → MAXIM: The recommended financial infrastructure strategy (Mercury banking → expedited D-U-N-S → sequenced tradelines → store credit → Amex/Cap One → conservative tax posture) traces to the Root Thesis Maxim as follows: (1) 'Sovereign systems' — A bank account under the entity's EIN, business credit profile under the entity's D-U-N-S, and business credit cards that do not burden the member's personal credit profile collectively create financial sovereignty. The DAO can transact, engage counterparties, and fund operations without routing through the member's personal financial infrastructure. (2) 'Self-sustaining systems' — The net-30 tradeline strategy, executed within the $200/month discretionary budget and filled with legitimate business deductions, creates a credit profile that generates compounding optionality (store credit → business credit cards → future financing lines) at near-zero net cost after deductions. The tradeline program is self-funding through the deduction value it generates. (3) 'Compound human agency' — Every credit tier achieved unlocks the next: tradelines → PAYDEX → store credit → business cards → future business lines of credit. Each layer compounds the member's operational agency — the ability to engage contractors without cash-on-hand, purchase equipment on credit, separate business and personal liability, and ultimately scale the DAO's operations without proportional personal capital commitment. (4) 'Over generational timescales' — The conservative tax posture, clean audit record, and Section 183 mitigation strategy ensure the DAO remains legally sound across the 3-of-5 profit-intent horizon and beyond. The governance documentation system (this proposal and all subsequent proposals) creates an on-chain record of business purpose that constitutes durable evidence of profit intent — a generational asset. The Ostrom Principle 1 (clearly defined boundaries) is satisfied by the bank account establishing the DAO's financial boundary; Principle 4 (monitoring) is satisfied by Mercury's API access enabling programmatic financial governance monitoring. The Buffett capital allocation principle is satisfied by sequencing investments for maximum compounding return (accelerated D-U-N-S → accelerated PAYDEX → accelerated credit access) against minimal capital deployment ($1,850 incremental over 12 months). The DAO LLC that emerges from this 12-month program will have: a primary operating account with API access, a PAYDEX score of 80+, 5+ reporting tradelines, 2-3 store credit accounts, 2 business credit cards, and a clean Schedule C deduction posture — all for approximately $2,515 total cash deployment. This is a high-return infrastructure investment directly enabling the creation of sovereign, self-sustaining, compounding human agency.

deferredDecisions.trigger_condition: First token revenue recognized AND monthly documented business-use driving exceeds 400 miles AND specific operational contract requiring regular travel is executed
deferredDecisions.review_date: Revisit at Proposal #006 or upon trigger condition, whichever is earlier

deferredDecisions.trigger_condition: Month 8 minimum AND PAYDEX 80+ AND at least $1 revenue recognized on Schedule C
deferredDecisions.review_date: Proposal review at month 8 of credit-building program

deferredDecisions.trigger_condition: Member must document exclusive, regular use of a defined workspace area used solely for DAO business; current 'shared workspace constraints' note in proposal disqualifies without modification
deferredDecisions.review_date: Annual tax planning review for 2026 Schedule C

deferredDecisions.trigger_condition: PAYDEX 80+, 12+ months of credit history, first revenue recognized, specific operational need identified
CRITICAL: Section 183 clock is running from 2026. Recommend Proposal #004 or #005 within 90 days to establish formal token revenue generation timeline targeting first revenue by Q4 2027 at latest
deferredDecisions.review_date: Within 90 days of this proposal ratification

uncertaintiesAndLimitations: Mercury may decline to open an account for a DAO LLC due to crypto-adjacent classification. If declined, Relay is the approved backup. The Human Executor should disclose DAO LLC structure proactively and have Articles of Organization, Operating Agreement, and EIN confirmation ready.

D&B tradeline reporting timelines are not guaranteed — vendor reporting lags of 30-60 days after first paid invoice are common. The PAYDEX score may not appear for 60-90 days after first payment. All timeline estimates in the roadmap are approximate.

The IRS has not issued formal guidance on DAO LLC tax treatment beyond disregarded entity status for single-member LLCs. Wyoming's statutory framework provides legal recognition, but Section 183 hobby loss analysis may be applied by an examiner unfamiliar with DAO structures. Strong documentation of business purpose through this governance system is the primary mitigation.

At $0 revenue, store credit and business credit card approvals are not guaranteed. Approval depends on PAYDEX score at application time, vendor underwriting policies, and any requirement for personal guarantee. The Human Executor should be prepared to provide personal guarantee for early applications and negotiate removal upon revenue establishment.

The $200/month discretionary budget creates a tight constraint. If any single tradeline vendor imposes higher minimum order requirements than anticipated, the budget must be reallocated across fewer vendors rather than breached.
